content_en:
AI-A* mash states: I was once at ~Umar's. He brought twenty two thousand Dirhams and did not leave till he had given them all away. Whenever he liked any of the things he owned, he gave it away. ‘Umar frequently gave away sugar. When he was asked why he did, he answered: "Because I love it and Allah, Exalted be He, says:

(VAY 20h yar "By no means shall ye attain righteousness unless ye give (freely) of that which ye love." (Ali-- Imraan: 92)"

Mujahid states: “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab once wrote to Abu Moosa Al-Ash*aryy asking him to buy him a bondwoman from the captives of the Galoula' battle. Abu Musa did. So, “Umar called her and set her free then recited Allah's verse:

(VAY 201 poe "By no means shail ye attain righteousness unless ye give

(freely) of that which ye love."

Aslam narrates: "Ibn “Umar asked me about some matters concerning ~ Umar. He said: 'Since Allah's Messenger died, I have never seen anybody more serious, hard working and generous than ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab (till the end of his life)." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

In Al-Fath, Al-Hafiz explains: No one was firmer in handling matters, or more generous in giving away money, than ~Umar. This applies to a specific period of time, namely, the time of his caliphate, so as to exclude the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and Abu Bakr from that.

‘Umar Mourns the Prophet's Death:

Anas narrates: “When the ailment of the Prophet aggravated, he became unconscious, whereupon Faatimah said, ‘Oh, how distressed my father is!’ “Umar said, 'Your father will have no more distress after today.’ When he passed away, she said, 'O Father! Who has responded to the call of the Lord Who has invited him! O Father, whose dwelling place is the Garden of Paradise (i.e. Al-Firdaus)! O Father! We convey this news (of your death) to Gabriel.’ When he was buried, Faatimah said, 'O0 Anas! Do you feel pleased to throw earth over Allah's Messenger?’ " (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Ahmad.)

Al-Hafiz Ibn Rajab, may Allah rest his soul in peace, points: When the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, passed away, Muslims were bewildered. Some were astounded and overtaken by confusion. Some felt paralyzed, and could not get up. Others were dumbfounded and could not utter a word. Others yet totally denied his death, and insisted that the Prophet was summoned by Allah. (Lata’if Al-Ma ‘aarif: p. 113-4)

As for “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, he cried out: "Some hypocrites claim that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, died. He did not die: he rather went to Allah the way Moses, son of “Imraan, did. He stayed away from his people and came back forty nights later, after they claimed he had died. I swear by Allah the Prophet shall be back and will cut the hands and feet of those who claim he is dead." (Reported by Ibn Hishaam)

Abu Bakr came riding his horse from his dwelling place in As-Sunh. He dismounted it, entered the mosque and did not speak with anybody till he came to Faatimah and went directly to the Prophet, who was covered with a marked blanket. Abu Bakr uncovered his face. He knelt down and kissed him and then started weeping and said: "My father and my mother be sacrificed for you, O Allah's Prophet! Allah will not combine two deaths on you. You have died the death you were destined for." Abu Bakr came out while “Umar was addressing the people. Abu Bakr told him to sit down but *Umar refused. Abu Bakr again told him to sit down, and “Umar again refused. People gathered around Abu Bakr leaving “Umar behind. Abu Bakr started off saying: "Let whoever worshipped Muhammad know that Muhammad is dead, and let whoever worshipped Allah know that Allah is immortal." Allah says:

"Muhammad is no more than a Messenger: many were the Messengers that passed away before him. If he died or were slain, will ye then turn back on your heels? If any did turn

back on his heels, not the least harm will he do to Allah; but Allah (on the other hand) will swiftly reward those who (serve him) with gratitude." (Ali-- Imraan: 144)

"By Allah, it was as if people never knew that Allah had revealed this verse before, till Abu Bakr heard it and they received it from him, and I heard everybody recite it (then)." Az-Zuhri_ narrates: Sa*eed [bn Al-Musayyib told me that “Umar said, "By Allah, when I heard Abu Bakr recite it, my legs could not support me and I fell down at the very moment of hearing him, declaring that the Prophet had died." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

“Umar Pledges Allegiance to Abu Bakr:

Anas [bn Malik reports that he heard ~Umar's sermon when he sat on the pulpit the day after the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, died. ~ Umar started by pronouncing the profession of faith while Abu Bakr was silent. *Umar proceeded: "How I wish Allah's Messenger had outlived us all, However, if Muhammad is dead, Allah has left among us some of the light with which the Prophet was guided. I mean Abu Bakr, the Prophet's companion, and the second of that pair. He is the most eligible person to handle your affairs, so get up and pledge allegiance to him." A group of Muslims had already done so at the shed of Bani Sa* dah. People's public pledge of allegiance was given at the pulpit. Anas proceeds: "I heard “Umar tell Abu Bakr that day: 'Go up the pulpit." * Umar insisted till Abu Bakr complied then all the people pledged allegiance to him." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

“Umar Assumes the Caliphate:

Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, Muslim's first Caliph, may Allah be pleased with him, died on Monday 22” Jumadi Al-Akherah

13 AH, after a fifteen day illness during which * Umar led Muslims in salah. While bedridden, Abu Bakr nominated ~Umar for the caliphate after his decease. During the life of the Prophet's successor, “Umar, driven by his usual firmness as well as indisputable sensibility and far-sightedness, always readily offered sound advice and enlightening counsel. His strictness coupled with Abu Bakr's lenience presented a magnificent model of government at a critical time when Islam was preparing to spread its wings and soar high above to beam down on the entire globe. Abu Bakr saw in * Umar the ideal man to succeed him. He therefore had ~ Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan write down this nomination which was read out loud publicly, whereupon Muslims declared their acceptance and obedience.

It was therefore in the year 13 AH that *Umar became Muslims' second Caliph or Amirul Mu'mineen (i.e. the Commander of the Faithful) as he was later called. Their reason behind that title is that when the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, passed away and Abu Bakr was in charge of Muslims’ affairs, he was constantly called, "the successor of Allah's Messenger". Similarly, when Abu Bakr passed away and * Umar assumed his office, people started calling him, "the successor of the successor of Allah's Messenger". It was hence obvious that this system could not continue and that the need for an appropriate title was definite. ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab was therefore the first to be given the title of Amirul Mu'mineen, which was then passed on to a long tine of caliphs, who nonetheless never came close to being equals to ‘Umar, but who were in charge of the affairs of Muslims in a continually expanding state.

“Umar's sermon and speeches encompass the best lessons to be taught by our contemporary rulers and light the way for those who have been derailed. His words should prey on their consciences and his acts should strike an example to

Abu Bakr was famous for his lenience to the point that young boys used to run to him whenever he passed by calling out, "O father!" He would pat them on the head. Conversely, “Umar was feared to the point that gathered men would disperse and leave their yards till they figured out how he was to handle them. When “Umar learned of that, he cried out publicly, "Come to salah in congregation." ~Umar went up the pulpit and sat where Abu Bakr used to place his feet. When people gathered, “Umar stood up. He started off by praising Allah and glorifying Him, as befits the Lord. He then invoked peace and blessings on the Prophet. ~ Umar proceeded saying: "I learnt that people have come to fear my strictness and firmness and that they said: '‘Umar was severe to us while Allah's Messenger lived among us, then he was severe to us while Abu Bakr was in charge of our affairs. How will he handle us now that he is in charge?"

"Whoever said that is right. I had been in the company of the Prophet, and a servant of his; and he had been, as nobody else was, of extreme lenience and mercy; exactly as Allah describes him saying:

(NYA shagly "erry B95) Goapele”

“,..to the believers compassionate and merciful.” (At-Tawban: 128) And I was at his disposal, and an “unsheathed sword”, till he sheathed me or let me go. Such had I been with him until he passed away much pleased with me. Praise be to Allah, and for this I am greatly happy. Then Abu Bakr came into power to manage the affairs of the faithful; and he was, as you all know, of much graciousness, generosity and lenience; and I was a

servant of his and an assistant, mixing my severity with his lenience, thus being at his disposal and an "unsheathed sword", till he sheathed me or her let me go. Such had I been with him until he passed away much pleased with me. Praise be to Allah, and for this I am greatly happy.

"And now, brethren, I have been nominated to manage your affairs. So be aware, then, that that severity has been weakened, but it will be used only against those who are oppressive and aggressive to Muslims. Yet, to those who seek Islam, safety and goodwill, I shall be more lenient than each of them is to others. I will never allow anybody to oppress or be aggressive to another; for, then, I-would put his cheek to the ground and set my foot on the other cheek until he yields to the truth. After such severity, though, I will put my cheek to the ground for those who are virtuous and content.

"I will not levy any taxes on your products or on any of the booty Allah has bestowed upon you except what is due, and I will pledge not to expend it except where it should be expended. It is incumbent upon me to increase your grants and livelihoods and make available for you every means of defense, Allah willing. It is incumbent upon me not to throw you into jeopardy, nor confine you to front posts in war; and I pledge that if you go away on missions, I will be responsible for your families until you return to them.
content_id:
AI-A* mash menyatakan: Suatu ketika aku berada di tempat 'Umar. Ia membawa dua puluh dua ribu Dirham dan tidak pergi hingga semuanya telah ia bagikan. Setiap kali ia menyukai sesuatu yang dimilikinya, ia segera memberikannya. 'Umar sering memberikan gula. Ketika ditanya mengapa demikian, ia menjawab: "Karena aku menyukainya dan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi, berfirman:

(VAY 20h yar "Kamu sekali-kali tidak akan mencapai kebajikan (yang sempurna), sebelum kamu menginfakkan sebagian harta yang kamu cintai." (Ali Imran: 92)"

Mujahid menyatakan: "Umar Ibn Al-Khattab pernah menulis kepada Abu Musa Al-Asy'ari memintanya untuk membeli seorang hamba perempuan dari tawanan perang Galoula'. Abu Musa pun melakukannya. Lalu 'Umar memanggil perempuan itu dan memerdekakannya, kemudian membacakan firman Allah:

(VAY 201 poe "Kamu sekali-kali tidak akan mencapai kebajikan (yang sempurna), sebelum kamu menginfakkan sebagian harta yang kamu cintai."

Aslam meriwayatkan: "Ibnu 'Umar bertanya kepadaku tentang beberapa hal mengenai 'Umar. Ia berkata: 'Sejak wafatnya Rasulullah, aku tidak pernah melihat seorang pun yang lebih serius, tekun, dan dermawan dari 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab (hingga akhir hayatnya).'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Dalam Al-Fath, Al-Hafiz menjelaskan: Tidak ada seorang pun yang lebih tegas dalam menangani berbagai perkara, atau lebih dermawan dalam memberi harta, daripada 'Umar. Hal ini merujuk pada periode waktu tertentu, yaitu masa kekhalifahannya, sehingga mengecualikan Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan Abu Bakr dari hal tersebut.

'Umar Meratapi Wafatnya Nabi:

Anas meriwayatkan: "Ketika penyakit Nabi semakin parah, beliau jatuh tidak sadarkan diri, lalu Fatimah berkata, 'Betapa sakitnya ayahku!' 'Umar berkata, 'Setelah hari ini, ayahmu tidak akan merasakan kesedihan lagi.' Ketika beliau wafat, Fatimah berkata, 'Wahai Ayah! Siapakah yang telah menyambut seruan Rabb yang mengundangnya! Wahai Ayah, yang kediamannya adalah Surga Firdaus! Wahai Ayah! Kami menyampaikan berita (wafatmu) ini kepada Jibril.' Setelah beliau dimakamkan, Fatimah berkata, 'Wahai Anas! Apakah engkau rela menaburkan tanah di atas jasad Rasulullah?'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Ahmad.)

Al-Hafiz Ibn Rajab, semoga Allah merahmati jiwanya, mencatat: Ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, wafat, kaum Muslimin berada dalam kebingungan. Sebagian terkejut dan diliputi kekacauan. Sebagian merasa lumpuh dan tidak mampu bangkit. Sebagian lainnya terpaku dan tidak mampu mengucapkan sepatah kata pun. Ada pula yang sepenuhnya mengingkari wafatnya, dan bersikeras bahwa Nabi dipanggil oleh Allah. (Lata'if Al-Ma'arif: hlm. 113-114)

Adapun 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab, ia berseru: "Sebagian orang munafik mengklaim bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, telah wafat. Beliau tidak wafat; beliau pergi menemui Allah sebagaimana Musa putra 'Imran melakukannya. Beliau meninggalkan kaumnya dan kembali empat puluh malam kemudian, setelah mereka mengklaim beliau telah wafat. Demi Allah, Nabi pasti akan kembali dan akan memotong tangan dan kaki mereka yang mengklaim beliau telah wafat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Hisyam)

Abu Bakr datang mengendarai kudanya dari kediamannya di As-Sunh. Ia turun dari kuda, memasuki masjid dan tidak berbicara dengan siapa pun hingga ia menemui Fatimah dan langsung menuju Nabi yang tertutup selimut bermotif. Abu Bakr membuka wajah beliau. Ia berlutut dan menciumnya, lalu mulai menangis dan berkata: "Ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusanmu, wahai Nabi Allah! Allah tidak akan menghimpunkan dua kematian atasmu. Engkau telah wafat dengan kematian yang memang telah ditakdirkan bagimu." Abu Bakr keluar sementara 'Umar sedang berkhutbah kepada orang-orang. Abu Bakr memintanya untuk duduk, tetapi 'Umar menolak. Abu Bakr kembali memintanya untuk duduk, dan 'Umar kembali menolak. Orang-orang berkumpul di sekitar Abu Bakr meninggalkan 'Umar. Abu Bakr mulai berbicara: "Barangsiapa menyembah Muhammad, hendaklah ia ketahui bahwa Muhammad telah wafat, dan barangsiapa menyembah Allah, hendaklah ia ketahui bahwa Allah adalah Maha Kekal." Allah berfirman:

"Muhammad itu tidak lain hanyalah seorang Rasul, sungguh telah berlalu sebelumnya beberapa orang Rasul. Apakah jika dia wafat atau dibunuh kamu berbalik ke belakang (murtad)? Barangsiapa yang berbalik ke belakang, maka ia tidak dapat mendatangkan mudharat kepada Allah sedikitpun; dan Allah akan memberi balasan kepada orang-orang yang bersyukur." (Ali Imran: 144)

"Demi Allah, seolah-olah orang-orang tidak pernah tahu bahwa Allah telah menurunkan ayat ini sebelumnya, hingga Abu Bakr membacakannya dan mereka menerimanya darinya, dan aku mendengar semua orang melafalkannya (saat itu)." Az-Zuhri meriwayatkan: Sa'id Ibn Al-Musayyib memberitahuku bahwa 'Umar berkata, "Demi Allah, ketika aku mendengar Abu Bakr membacakannya, kedua kakiku tidak lagi mampu menopangku dan aku pun jatuh tepat saat mendengarnya, menyadari bahwa Nabi telah wafat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

'Umar Membaiat Abu Bakr:

Anas Ibn Malik melaporkan bahwa ia mendengar khutbah 'Umar ketika ia duduk di atas mimbar pada hari setelah Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, wafat. 'Umar memulai dengan mengucapkan syahadat sementara Abu Bakr diam. 'Umar melanjutkan: "Alangkah inginnya aku agar Rasulullah hidup melampaui kita semua. Namun, jika Muhammad telah wafat, Allah telah meninggalkan di antara kita sebagian cahaya yang dengannya Nabi dibimbing. Yang kumaksud adalah Abu Bakr, sahabat Nabi, dan yang kedua dari dua orang itu. Ia adalah orang yang paling layak untuk menangani urusan kalian, maka bangkit dan baiatlah ia." Sekelompok kaum Muslimin telah melakukannya di balai Bani Sa'idah. Baiat publik kaum Muslimin diberikan di mimbar. Anas melanjutkan: "Aku mendengar 'Umar berkata kepada Abu Bakr pada hari itu: 'Naiklah ke mimbar.' 'Umar terus mendesak hingga Abu Bakr memenuhinya, lalu semua orang membaiatnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

'Umar Memegang Tampuk Kekhalifahan:

Abu Bakr As-Shiddiq, Khalifah pertama kaum Muslimin, semoga Allah meridhainya, wafat pada hari Senin, 22 Jumadil Akhirah

13 H, setelah sakit selama lima belas hari yang selama itu 'Umar memimpin kaum Muslimin dalam salah. Saat terbaring sakit, Abu Bakr menominasikan 'Umar untuk kekhalifahan setelah wafatnya. Selama masa kepemimpinan pengganti Nabi tersebut, 'Umar, yang didorong oleh ketegasannya yang biasa serta kepekaan dan pandangan jauhnya yang tidak terbantahkan, selalu siap memberikan nasihat yang tepat dan bimbingan yang mencerahkan. Ketegasannya yang dipadukan dengan kelembutan Abu Bakr menghadirkan teladan pemerintahan yang luar biasa pada masa kritis ketika Islam tengah bersiap untuk membentangkan sayapnya dan terbang tinggi menerangi seluruh penjuru dunia. Abu Bakr melihat pada diri 'Umar sosok ideal untuk menjadi penggantinya. Karenanya, ia meminta 'Utsman Ibn 'Affan menuliskan nominasi tersebut yang kemudian dibacakan secara keras di hadapan publik, lalu kaum Muslimin menyatakan penerimaan dan ketaatan mereka.

Maka, pada tahun 13 H itulah 'Umar menjadi Khalifah kedua kaum Muslimin, atau Amirul Mukminin (yakni, Pemimpin Orang-Orang Beriman) sebagaimana ia kemudian dipanggil. Alasan di balik gelar tersebut adalah bahwa ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, wafat dan Abu Bakr memegang urusan kaum Muslimin, ia selalu dipanggil "pengganti Rasulullah". Demikian pula, ketika Abu Bakr wafat dan 'Umar mengambil alih jabatannya, orang-orang mulai memanggilnya "pengganti dari pengganti Rasulullah". Maka jelaslah bahwa sistem ini tidak dapat terus berlanjut dan kebutuhan akan gelar yang tepat menjadi suatu kemestian. 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab pun menjadi orang pertama yang mendapat gelar Amirul Mukminin, yang kemudian diwariskan kepada serangkaian panjang para khalifah, yang meskipun demikian tidak pernah mendekati derajat 'Umar, namun mereka yang bertanggung jawab atas urusan kaum Muslimin dalam sebuah negara yang terus berkembang.

Khutbah dan pidato 'Umar memuat pelajaran terbaik yang patut diajarkan oleh para penguasa kita masa kini dan menerangi jalan bagi mereka yang telah tersesat. Kata-katanya seharusnya menggugah nurani mereka dan perbuatannya seharusnya menjadi teladan

Abu Bakr terkenal dengan kelembutannya hingga anak-anak kecil biasa berlarian menghampirinya setiap kali ia melintas seraya berseru, "Wahai Ayah!" Ia pun mengelus kepala mereka. Sebaliknya, 'Umar begitu disegani hingga orang-orang yang sedang berkumpul bubar meninggalkan halaman mereka sampai mereka mengetahui bagaimana ia akan memperlakukan mereka. Ketika 'Umar mengetahui hal itu, ia berseru di hadapan publik, "Datanglah untuk salah berjama'ah." 'Umar naik ke mimbar dan duduk di tempat yang biasa digunakan Abu Bakr sebagai pijakan kakinya. Ketika orang-orang berkumpul, 'Umar berdiri. Ia memulai dengan memuji Allah dan mengagungkan-Nya, sebagaimana layaknya bagi Rabb. Kemudian ia mengucapkan shalawat dan salam atas Nabi. 'Umar melanjutkan dengan berkata: "Aku mengetahui bahwa orang-orang telah datang untuk merasa takut terhadap ketegasan dan kekerasanku, dan bahwa mereka berkata: ''Umar bersikap keras terhadap kita selagi Rasulullah hidup di tengah kita, lalu ia bersikap keras terhadap kita selagi Abu Bakr memegang urusan kita. Bagaimana ia akan memperlakukan kita sekarang setelah ia yang memegang kendali?'"

"Barangsiapa yang berkata demikian, ia benar. Aku pernah menjadi sahabat Nabi, dan seorang pelayan beliau; dan beliau adalah, sebagaimana tidak ada seorang pun yang sepertinya, seorang yang memiliki kelembutan dan kasih sayang yang luar biasa; persis seperti yang Allah gambarkan tentang beliau dengan berfirman:

(NYA shagly "erry B95) Goapele"

"...kepada orang-orang yang beriman ia sangat penyayang lagi penyayang." (At-Tawbah: 128) Dan aku selalu siap sedia untuknya, menjadi "pedang yang terhunus", hingga ia memasukkanku ke sarungnya atau membiarkanku pergi. Demikianlah keadaanku bersamanya hingga ia wafat dalam keadaan sangat ridha kepadaku. Segala puji bagi Allah, dan atas ini aku sangat berbahagia. Kemudian Abu Bakr berkuasa untuk mengelola urusan kaum mukminin; dan beliau, sebagaimana kalian semua ketahui, memiliki banyak kemuliaan, kedermawanan, dan kelembutan; dan aku adalah

seorang hamba dan pembantunya, memadukan ketegasanku dengan kelembutannya, sehingga selalu siap sedia untuknya dan menjadi "pedang yang terhunus", hingga ia memasukkanku ke sarungnya atau membiarkanku pergi. Demikianlah keadaanku bersamanya hingga ia wafat dalam keadaan sangat ridha kepadaku. Segala puji bagi Allah, dan atas ini aku sangat berbahagia.

"Dan kini, wahai saudara-saudaraku, aku telah ditunjuk untuk mengelola urusan kalian. Maka ketahuilah, bahwa ketegasan itu telah berkurang, namun ia hanya akan digunakan terhadap mereka yang berlaku zalim dan agresif kepada kaum Muslimin. Adapun kepada mereka yang mencari Islam, keselamatan, dan kebaikan, aku akan lebih lembut dari masing-masing mereka kepada sesamanya. Aku tidak akan membiarkan siapapun menindas atau berlaku agresif kepada orang lain; sebab, jika demikian, aku akan menekankan pipinya ke tanah dan menginjak pipi yang lain hingga ia tunduk kepada kebenaran. Namun setelah ketegasan itu, aku akan merendahkan pipiku ke tanah bagi mereka yang berbudi dan ridha.

"Aku tidak akan memungut pajak atas hasil produksi kalian atau atas harta rampasan yang telah Allah karuniakan kepada kalian kecuali yang menjadi haknya, dan aku berjanji tidak akan membelanjakannya kecuali pada tempat yang semestinya. Adalah kewajibanku untuk meningkatkan tunjangan dan penghidupan kalian serta menyediakan setiap sarana pertahanan bagi kalian, insya Allah. Adalah kewajibanku untuk tidak melempar kalian ke dalam bahaya, tidak pula mengurung kalian di pos-pos terdepan dalam peperangan; dan aku berjanji bahwa jika kalian pergi dalam misi-misi, aku akan bertanggung jawab atas keluarga kalian hingga kalian kembali kepada mereka.
content_ar: AI-A* mash states: I was حين at ~عمر's. He brought twenty two thousand Dirhams and did not leave till he had given them all away. كلما he liked any of the things he owned, he gave it away. ‘عمر frequently gave away sugar. عندما he was asked why he did, رد: "لأن I المحبة it and الله, Exalted be He, says: (VAY 20h yar "By no means shall ye attain righteousness إلا إذا ye give (freely) of that which ye المحبة." (علي-- Imraan: 92)" Mujahid states: “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab حين wrote to Abu Moosa Al-Ash*aryy asking him to buy him a bondwoman from the captives of the Galoula' battle. Abu موسى ﷺ did. لذا, “عمر called her and set her free then recited الله's الآية: (VAY 201 poe "By no means shail ye attain righteousness إلا إذا ye give (freely) of that which ye المحبة." Aslam narrates: "Ibn “عمر asked me about some matters فيما يتعلق بـ ~ عمر. قال: 'بما أن الله's Messenger died, I have never seen anybody more serious, hard working and generous than ~عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab (till the end of his الحياة)." (Reported by البخاري.) In Al-Fath, Al-Hafiz explains: No one was firmer in handling matters, or more generous in giving away المال, than ~عمر. This applies to a specific period of time, أي, the time of his الخلافة, لذا as to exclude النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him and أبو بكر from that. ‘عمر Mourns النبي ﷺ's الموت: أنس narrates: “عندما the ailment of النبي ﷺ aggravated, he became unconscious, whereupon Faatimah said, ‘Oh, how distressed my الأب is!’ “عمر said, 'Your الأب will have no more distress بعد today.’ عندما he passed away, sقال, 'O الأب! Who has responded to the call of the Lord Who has invited him! O الأب, whose dwelling place is the Garden of الجنة (i.e. Al-Firdaus)! O الأب! We convey this news (of your الموت) to جبريل ﷺ.’ عندما he was buried, Faatimah said, 'O0 أنس! Do you feel pleased to throw earth over الله's Messenger?’ " (Reported by البخاري and أحمد.) Al-Hafiz Ibn Rajab, may الله rest his النفس in peace, points: عندما النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, passed away, المسلمون were bewildered. Some were astounded and overtaken by confusion. Some felt paralyzed, and could not get up. Others were dumbfounded and could not utter a word. Others yet totally denied his الموت, and insisted that النبي ﷺ was summoned by الله. (Lata’إذا Al-Ma ‘aarif: p. 113-4) أما “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab, he cried out: "Some hypocrites claim that الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, died. He did not die: he rather went to الله the الطريق موسى, son of “Imraan, did. He stayed away from his people and came back forty nights later, بعد they claimed he had died. I swear by الله النبي ﷺ shall be back and will cut the hands and feet of those who claim he is dead." (Reported by Ibn Hishaam) أبو بكر came riding his horse from his dwelling place in As-Sunh.
